 {"id":"bd-sf6w","title":"Bitmap CRC: verify kernel bitmap pre-processing before checksumming","description":"Read the Linux kernel source for ext4_block_bitmap_csum_set() and ext4_block_bitmap_csum_verify() to determine:
(a) Whether the kernel zeroes unused tail bits in the bitmap before checksumming. For a group with fewer blocks than blocks_per_group, the tail bits may be set to 1 (indicating 'used') by mkfs but the kernel may ignore/zero them before CRC.
(b) Whether the kernel checksums exactly block_size bytes or clusters_per_group/8 bytes. Our code checksums the full block.
(c) Whether the kernel XORs or masks any fields in the group descriptor before storing the checksum.

SPECIFIC FILES TO CHECK:
- fs/ext4/balloc.c: ext4_block_bitmap_csum_set(), ext4_block_bitmap_csum_verify()
- fs/ext4/bitmap.c: ext4_bitmap_csum()
- include/linux/ext4.h: struct ext4_group_desc layout

ACCEPTANCE: Document the exact kernel pre-processing steps. If any differ from our implementation, fix our code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:25.650024112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:28.787877259Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:28.787389015Z","close_reason":"Kernel source confirmed: no group number in bitmap CRC, length is clusters_per_group/8 for blocks and inodes_per_group/8 for inodes. Fixed in commit 20d65f4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crc32c","ext4","investigation"]}
 {"id":"bd-sfb9","title":"Add proptest property-based tests for ffs-fuse","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:04:17.207361401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:33:52.078831108Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:33:52.078801833Z","close_reason":"Added 33 proptest property-based tests covering inode routing, xattr reply classification, setxattr mode parsing, xattr name encoding, AccessPredictor bounds/eviction, ReadaheadManager insert/take/eviction, AtomicMetrics consistency, MountOptions resolution, PerCoreConfig/PerCoreDispatcher routing/metrics, CoreMetrics hit rate, and imbalance ratio. All 120 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-sik","title":"ffs-types: Checked arithmetic + alignment helpers (block math, offsets)","description":"Goal: centralize all tricky integer math used by on-disk parsing and block mapping.

Deliverables:
- checked_add_bytes(offset, len) -> ByteOffset end
- checked_mul_block(block_nr, block_size) -> ByteOffset
- align_down/align_up helpers with overflow checking
- conversions with explicit error paths (no silent truncation)

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover overflow/edge cases.
- Call sites in ffs-block/ffs-ondisk/ffs-core prefer these helpers over ad-hoc math.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:24.565357285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T08:41:43.432870696Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T08:41:43.432852562Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}

@@ -167,6 +167,9 @@ overwrites, with no root-entry, source-child, or file-byte drift.
 Mounted-path btrfs cross-parent directory rename coverage now also freezes
 parent `st_nlink` accounting: moving a child directory decrements the source
 parent, increments the destination parent, and preserves the moved inode.
+Mounted-path btrfs hard-link unlink coverage now also freezes `st_nlink`
+accounting: removing one hard-link alias deletes only that directory entry,
+preserves the surviving inode/data, and restores the survivor link count to 1.
 Mounted-path btrfs hard-link refusal coverage now also freezes exact `EPERM`
 for directory sources, `ENOTDIR` for non-directory parents, and `EEXIST` for
 occupied destinations, with no dirent or source-`st_nlink` drift.
